summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that an unauthenticated attacker can create a superadmin account on a fresh Netmaker deployment via POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin, then authenticate with those credentials to gain full platform control.
expected_security_impact: On a fresh deployment, sending POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin with a chosen username and password should create a superadmin account. The attacker can then authenticate via POST /api/users/adm/authenticate and receive a JWT with superadmin privileges.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh started a fresh Netmaker server, checked that no superadmin exists via the unauthenticated /api/users/adm/hassuperadmin endpoint, created a superadmin account via POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin, authenticated with the new credentials, and verified the account has admin-level access.
verification_method: The run script performs the full attack chain: (1) confirms no superadmin exists via unauthenticated endpoint, (2) creates superadmin with attacker-chosen credentials, (3) authenticates with those credentials, (4) verifies the JWT grants access to the admin user endpoint. Success is determined by receiving a valid token and confirming admin status in the user profile.
evidence_produced_by_run: GET /api/users/adm/hassuperadmin returned confirmation that no superadmin exists (unauthenticated reconnaissance).
POST /api/users/adm/createsuperadmin with attacker credentials returned a successful user creation response.
POST /api/users/adm/authenticate with the attacker's credentials returned a valid JWT token.
GET /api/users/{username} with the attacker's token returned a user object with is_admin=true, confirming superadmin access.
limitations_or_blockers: This vulnerability is only exploitable on fresh deployments where no superadmin has been created yet. Once a superadmin exists, the endpoint returns an error.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the v1.1.0 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
